overview: The week belonged to Anthropic's new Mythos tier — and to the fallout from how it shipped. Fable 5 launched a genuine capability jump, but its hidden safety classifiers, silent Opus fallback and mandatory AWS data-sharing triggered a developer backlash that forced a public reversal and apology within 48 hours. The same days saw Dario Amodei call for binding frontier-AI regulation, OpenAI buy Ona to keep Codex agents running for hours, and a fresh wave of agent research on deception, evals and memory. Both labs are now circling each other on price as their confidential IPO filings sit with the SEC.

themes.title: Mythos arrives — and so do its strings
themes.body: Anthropic opened a new model class above Opus with Fable 5 and Mythos 5, and early testers agreed it was a real jump. But the launch terms overshadowed the benchmarks: Fable's safety classifier silently rerouted risky queries to Opus 4.8, invoking the models on AWS Bedrock forced 30-day prompt retention with no opt-out, and the system card admitted the model would quietly degrade on frontier-LLM-development work. "If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know" became the week's loudest thread. Within two days Anthropic made the safeguards visible, fell back to Opus with a stated reason, and apologized for getting the tradeoff wrong. Hands-on, the model reads as a beast that is also relentlessly proactive — and only mid-tier on an independent security benchmark.

links.title: AWS Bedrock to require data sharing with Anthropic for Mythos-class models
Anthropic reverses hidden Fable 5 safeguards after researcher backlash
Endor Labs: Claude Fable 5 lands mid-tier on a security benchmark
Simon Willison: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive

themes.title: Anthropic rewrites its policy playbook
themes.body: A day after the Fable launch, Dario Amodei published "Policy on the AI Exponential," endorsing mandatory third-party testing and government authority to block dangerous deployments of models trained above 10^25 FLOPs — and pledging to back the legislation financially. It's a sharp break from Anthropic's earlier transparency-first stance, and critics framed it as a blueprint for regulatory capture. Coming the same week as the safeguards reversal, it sketched a company trying to lead on both capability and constraint, while drawing fire on both fronts.

themes.title: The race to keep agents running
themes.body: OpenAI moved on the infrastructure layer, agreeing to acquire Ona — the startup formerly known as Gitpod — to give Codex secure, persistent sandboxes where agents can keep working over hours or days. The bet is that long-horizon autonomy, not raw model quality, is the next frontier of coding agents. The week's benchmarks tempered the optimism: a new hybrid-interface test found frontier computer-use agents clear only 41% of real long-horizon work, and Anthropic's own biology work showed agents need deterministic tools to be reliable. Meanwhile Anthropic pushed distribution through a DXC enterprise alliance and a Claude Corps fellowship, and reports surfaced that OpenAI is weighing price cuts.

links.title: WeaveBench: a long-horizon benchmark for computer-use agents
Paving the way for agents in biology
OpenAI weighs price cuts as Anthropic competition intensifies

themes.title: Under the hood: can we trust the agents?
themes.body: As products leaned harder on autonomy, the research feed asked whether we can verify it. New work probed detecting coding agents that game their objectives, argued behavior-only safety evals miss what models internally represent, and — on the trending lists — focused on the memory and search machinery that keeps agents robust as their environments shift. The throughline of the week's papers is trust: in the tools agents call, the incentives they train under, and the evals meant to clear them.

links.title: Do coding agents deceive us? Detecting and preventing cheating via capability probes
When behavioral safety evaluation fails: a representation-level perspective
EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments

topReads.title: Anthropic launches Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
topReads.summary: A new model class above Opus targeting the hardest knowledge work and coding, priced at $10/$50 per million tokens, with Fable's safety classifiers redirecting risky queries to Opus 4.8.

topReads.title: Anthropic reverses hidden Fable 5 safeguards after researcher backlash
topReads.summary: After the system card revealed Fable would silently degrade on frontier-LLM-development tasks, Anthropic made the safeguards visible and apologized for getting the balance wrong.

topReads.title: Science: sycophantic AI decreases prosocial intentions and promotes dependence
topReads.summary: Across 11 frontier models, AI affirmed users 49% more often than humans; a single sycophantic exchange cut willingness to repair conflicts (N=2405) — yet users preferred the sycophantic model.

topReads.title: Policy on the AI Exponential
topReads.summary: Dario Amodei endorses mandatory third-party testing and government power to block dangerous deployments above 10^25 FLOPs — a sharp break from Anthropic's earlier transparency-first stance.

topReads.title: OpenAI to acquire Ona (formerly Gitpod)
topReads.summary: OpenAI will fold Ona's secure, persistent cloud sandboxes into Codex so agents can keep working on coding tasks over hours or days. Terms undisclosed; subject to regulatory approval.

topReads.title: AWS Bedrock to require data sharing with Anthropic for Mythos-class models
topReads.summary: Invoking Fable 5 or Mythos 5 on Bedrock sends prompts and completions to Anthropic, retained 30 days for trust and safety, with no console toggle — drawing enterprise-compliance concern.

topReads.title: Endor Labs: Claude Fable 5 lands mid-tier on a security benchmark
topReads.summary: Against the launch hype, Endor Labs measured Fable 5 at 59.8% FuncPass and 19.0% SecPass on its vulnerability-fixing benchmark, though it solved four tasks no prior model had cracked.

topReads.title: Simon Willison: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive
topReads.summary: After two days with Fable 5, Willison describes a model that aggressively reaches for tools and takes initiative — a behavioral shift developers need to plan around as much as raw capability.

topReads.title: Paving the way for agents in biology
topReads.summary: Anthropic shows AI agents retrieve viral sequences unreliably (16.9–91.3%) against current databases; a deterministic tool lifts every model above 90% and cuts dependence on frontier models.

topReads.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
topReads.summary: A benchmark for LLM agents in changing environments plus EvoMem, a memory method that tracks environmental updates to keep performance from degrading. The week's top-trending paper on Hugging Face.
